---
title: "Getting USGS Streamflow Data into R in 5 Lines"
author: "Kyle Cahitas"
date: 2026-04-02
categories: [R, hydrology, tutorial]
description: "The dataRetrieval package makes NWIS access trivial. Here's the pattern I use for every watershed project."
---

## The Problem

Every hydrology project starts the same way — you need streamflow 
data, you go to the USGS NWIS website, you download a CSV manually. 
There's a better way.

## The Solution
```{{r}}
library(dataRetrieval)
library(tidyverse)

# Pull daily discharge for a site
flow <- readNWISdv(
  siteNumbers = "11143000",   # Big Sur River near Big Sur CA
  parameterCd = "00060",      # Discharge, cubic feet per second
  startDate = "2020-01-01",
  endDate = "2024-12-31"
) |>
  renameNWISColumns()

flow |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = Flow)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2c7bb6", linewidth = 0.4) +
  labs(
    title = "Big Sur River Daily Discharge",
    y = "Discharge (cfs)", x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

[Add your figure here once rendered]
